metadata.title: A Mighty Fortress Is Our God
metadata.subtitle: Ein feste Burg (Luther, 1529) — English tr. Frederic Henry Hedge (1853)
metadata.lyricist: Martin Luther (1483–1546), "Ein feste Burg ist unser Gott"; English translation by Frederic Henry Hedge (1805–1890)
metadata.x_pd_basis: Luther's original (d. 1546) is PD by > 470 years. Hedge's English translation (d. 1890) is PD by > 130 years
metadata.x_translator: Frederic Henry Hedge (English) — paired with 神はわがやぐら (Japanese, 1903)

chordpro:
{title: A Mighty Fortress Is Our God}
{subtitle: Ein feste Burg (Luther, 1529) — English tr. Frederic Henry Hedge, 1853}
{key: C}
{time: 4/4}
{tempo: 80}
{comment: Pair with 神はわがやぐら (Japanese translation, 讃美歌 1903). The "battle hymn of the Reformation."}

{start_of_verse: 1}
[C]A mighty [G]fortress [C]is our [G]God
[Am]A trusty [F]shield and [C]weapon[G]
[C]He helps us [F]free from [C]ev'ry [G]need
[Am]That hath us [Em]now o'er[F]taken.
[C]The old [F]evil [C]foe
[Am]Now means [Em]deadly [F]woe
[C]Deep guile [F]and great [C]might
[Am]Are his [Em]dread arms [F]in fight
[C]On earth [F]is not [C]his [G]equal.[C]
{end_of_verse}

{start_of_verse: 2}
[C]With might of [G]ours can [C]naught be [G]done
[Am]Soon were our [F]loss ef[C]fected[G]
[C]But for us [F]fights the [C]valiant [G]One
[Am]Whom God Him[Em]self e[F]lected.
[C]Ask ye, [F]Who is [C]this?
[Am]Jesus [Em]Christ it [F]is
[C]Of Saba[F]oth [C]Lord
[Am]And there's [Em]none other [F]God
[C]He holds [F]the field [C]for[G]ever.[C]
{end_of_verse}

{start_of_verse: 3}
[C]Though devils [G]all the [C]world should [G]fill
[Am]All eager [F]to de[C]vour us[G]
[C]We tremble [F]not, we [C]fear no [G]ill
[Am]They shall not [Em]over[F]pow'r us.
[C]This world's [F]prince may [C]still
[Am]Scowl fierce [Em]as he [F]will
[C]He can [F]harm us [C]none
[Am]He's judged; [Em]the deed [F]is done
[C]One little [F]word can [C]fell [G]him.[C]
{end_of_verse}

{start_of_verse: 4}
[C]The Word they [G]still shall [C]let re[G]main
[Am]Nor any [F]thanks have [C]for it[G]
[C]He's by our [F]side up[C]on the [G]plain
[Am]With His good [Em]gifts and [F]Spirit.
[C]And take they [F]our [C]life
[Am]Goods, fame, [Em]child, and [F]wife
[C]Though these [F]all be [C]gone
[Am]Our vict'[Em]ry has been [F]won
[C]The Kingdom [F]ours re[C]main[G]eth.[C]
{end_of_verse}
